trial_scientific_title_: Manipal Heart Failure Registry (MHFR): A Long Term, Prospective and Descriptive Study of Heart Failure in a Tertiary Care Hospital in South India
trial_indication: Heart Failure|Systolic Heart Failure|Diastolic Heart Failure|Decompensated Heart Failure|Acute Heart Failure
trial_description:
Burden of HF in India is approximate 2-5 million patients with an estimated prevalence of 2-3/1000 population. HF is responsible for approx1.8 million admissions annually in India. Very few studies are there in India which shows the detailed documentation of HF.

Manipal Heart failure Registry (MHFR) is an institutional level Heart Failure disease registry. In MHFR the investigator will document the detailed information about heart failure patients admitted or referred to Department of Cardiology, Kasturba Medical College, a constituent college and teaching hospital of Manipal University.

MHFR will include cohorts of patients with acute heart failure (AHF), with the intention of implementing a long-term follow-up, the use of health economic assessment to evaluate the degree of resource utilization; and quantifying the burden on quality of life for patients. Utilization of drug in HF patients will be evaluated as a part of this study to prevent the use of inappropriate drug therapy and to improve overall drug effectiveness and outcomes. Hence the protocol sought to identify the characteristics and long-term outcomes of Indian patients with acute heart failure admitted in an Indian tertiary care center in South India.

There are no risks to patients participating in the registry study, as standard of care quality will not be affected. It is unlikely that there will be any direct benefit as a result of participation in the HF-disease registry. However, the information contained within this non-interventional registry study will be used for research studies directed at improving the knowledge and treatment of acute heart failure as well as improving patients treatment in the future.

The potential impact of proposed research (Manipal Heart Failure Registry) is envisioned to be four-fold:

1. to enable a broad overview of the routine medical practices for HF treatment;
2. to assess the healthcare resource utilization and drug utilization review for heart failure patients
3. to assess the burden of disease (mortality, re-hospitalization) in the long term; and
4. to provide a novel overview of the impact of HF syndrome on quality of life as well as health economics.

trial_public_title: A Study to Determine the Validity and Sensitivity of 'Questionnaire to Screen for HYpothyroidism' (Q'SHY)
trial_scientific_title_: A Study to Determine the Validity and Sensitivity of 'Questionnaire to Screen for HYpothyroidism' (Q'SHY)
trial_intervention_name: DIAGNOSTIC_TEST: Blood test for Thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH)
trial_description:
Type of Study Questionnaire- Development and validation

Information about the Q'SHY:

* The Q'SHY- Questionnaire for Screening of Hypothyroidism is a Patient Reported Outcome (PRO) tool to enable screening for Hypothyroidism in the general population.
* It is a 20-item questionnaire which covers aspects of the symptoms of Hypothyroidism and incorporates the contributory factors from patient history which when combined, provides a robust tool to effectively screen the population.
* The questionnaire content is being created based on a thorough search for relevant literature and encompasses all important aspects for screening purposes

trial_scientific_title_: One Year Clinical Outcome Of Calcified Obstructive Coronary Artery Disease Treated With Rotablation And Drug Eluting Stent With The Use Of Intravascular Imaging
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	Rotablation Coronary Angiography and Stenting with Drug Eluting Stent 	1.Rotablation Frequency-160000 to 180000 rpm for burr less than 2 mm and 140000 to 160000 rpm for burr more than 2 mm Duration: Each run of 2-3 seconds with total run duration approximately 30 second 2.Coronary Angiography and Stenting with Drug Eluting Stent
trial_indication: (1) ICD-10 Condition: I251||Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery,
trial_description: This study is a prospective, single center, interventional study for one year to see the clinical outcome of calcified obstructive coronary artery disease treated with rotablation and drug eluting stents with the use of intravascular imaging, either OCT or IVUS. Primary end point will be MACE (Major Adverse Cardiovascular Events) as defined by death, MI and TLR, either percutaneous or surgical. Secondary outcome will be stent restenosis, stent thrombosis and TVR individually

trial_public_title: Use of risk scores to predict occurrence of bleeding events in patients undergoing heart blood vessel stenting
trial_scientific_title_: Use of risk scores to predict occurrence of bleeding events in patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention
trial_sponsors: Kasturba Medical College Manipal Academy of Higher Education Manipal
trial_indication: Kasturba Medical College Manipal Academy of Higher Education Manipal
trial_description:
1. Title of the Project: Use of risk scores to predict occurrence of bleeding events in patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention

2. Type of Study: Prospective, Observational, Single Centre Study

 

3. Aims & objectives:

 

Aims-

 

To assess the bleeding risk in patients undergoing Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI)

 

Objectives-

 

To compare the ARC-HBR definition with Precise-DAPT bleeding risk score in predicting major bleeding events in patients undergoing PCI.
To Calculate the incidence of major bleeding events, ischemic events and mortality MACE in patients undergoing PCI at 6 months follow up.
 
4. Justification for study:
There are many Atrial Fibrillation score that have been developed to predict bleeding risk in patients of Atrial Fibrillation. But there are very few scores that have been validated to predict the bleeding events in patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention. Those scoring system have been developed to facilitate risk stratification of patients but effectiveness to identify patient at high bleeding risk remains. Recently proposed ARC-HBR consensus was done to portend an incidence of bleeding, but this study was done in western population. So, this study is carried out to assess the bleeding events in patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention in Indian population. To our knowledge, no such studies are done to predict the high bleeding risk in patients undergoing PCI using the proposed ARC-HBR criteria in India. This study will also compare ARC-HBR and PRECISE-DAPT score as these two scores are not compared previously in predicting major bleedings in patients undergoing PCI.

 

5. Departments Involved: Cardiology

 

6. Study Period: 1.5 year

 

7. Sample Size: 455 patients. Sample size was estimated at 5% level of significance with an allowable error of 5%, using the following formula: 

 n = Z2 spe(1-spe)/e2(1-prevalance)
z-5 1.96 at 95% C.I

A minimum sample size of 395 cases will be required for present study.

 

8. Materials and Methods: 

 A) Inclusion Criteria:

 1.All patients >18 years who undergo PCI with DES for standard medication at Kasturba Hospital, Manipal during the study period.

 B) Exclusion criteria:

 1.All patients who undergo PCI with bare metal stent and stent grafts.

 C) Biological materials required:

 None. Only reports from standard blood tests will be noted. No additional blood samples will be used for the purpose of the study.

 

 D) Statistical Methods:

 

 Descriptive statistical analysis will be carried out. Chi-square test and Fisher Exact test will be used whenever the data presented in terms of frequency, and student t-test will be used to test significance when the results are presented as mean ± standard deviation (SD). Odds ratio (OR) and its confidence interval (CI) will be computed for event of interest. P value <0.05 will be considered statistically significant. The statistical software namely SPSS 22.0 will be used for the analysis of the data.

 

 E) Tools used:

 

1.ARC -HBR MAJOR AND MINOR CRITERIA AT THE TIME OF PCI1

 

MAJOR

MINOR

 

Age >_75 years

Anticipated use of long term oral anticoagulation

 

Severe or end stage CKD (eGFR <30 Ml/min)

Moderate CKD (eGFR 30-59 Ml/min)

Hemoglobin< 11 g/dl

Hemoglobin 11-12.9 g/dl for men and 11-11.9 g/dl for women

Spontaneous bleeding requiring hospitalisation or transfusion in the past 6 months or any time,if recurrent
Spontaneous bleeding requiring hospitalization or transfusion within the past 12 months not meeting the major criteria

Moderate or severe baseline thrombocytopenia

 

Chronic bleeding diasthesis

 

Liver cirrhosis with portal hypertension

 

 

 

 

 

 

Long term use of oral NSAIDS or steroids

 

Active malignancy(excluding nonmelanoma skin cancer)within the past 12 month

 

 

 

Previous spontaneous ICH (at any time)

Previous traumatic ich within the past 12 month

Presence of bAVM

Moderate or severe ischemic stroke within past 6 months

 

Any ischemic stroke at any time not meeting the major criterion

Nondeferrable major surgery on DAPT

 

 

Recent major surgery or major trauma within 30 days before PCI

 

 

 

2.PRECISE DAPT SCORE2

 

Hemoglobin

Age (years)

WBC (109/L)

Creatinine clearance (ml/min)

Prior bleeding

Web based risk calculator available at http://www.precisedaptscore.com/predapt/webcalculator.html

 

 

3.National Cardiovascular Data Registry CathPCI Registry (version 4.4)3

 

Intra and post-procedure events(complete for each cath lab visit)

 

 NO YES

Myocardial Infarction(positive biomarkers)

 

 

Cardiogenic shock

è If yes,size O < 3 cm O 3-5 cm O 5-10 cm O >10 cm

4. BARC DEFINITIONS4

 

TYPE 0

NO BLEEDING

TYPE 1

Bleeding that is not actionable and does not cause the patient to seek treatment

TYPE 2

Any clinically overt sign of hemorrhage that “is actionable” and requires diagnostic studies, hospitalization, or treatment by a health care professional

TYPE 3

a. Overt bleeding plus hemoglobin drop of 3 to < 5 g/dL (provided hemoglobin drop is related to bleed); transfusion with overt bleeding

 

b. Overt bleeding plus hemoglobin drop < 5 g/dL (provided hemoglobin drop is related to bleed); cardiac tamponade; bleeding requiring surgical intervention for control; bleeding requiring IV vasoactive agents

 

c. Intracranial hemorrhage confirmed by autopsy, imaging, or lumbar puncture; intraocular bleed compromising vision

TYPE 4

CABG-related bleeding within 48 hours

TYPE 5

a. Probable fatal bleeding

b.Definite fatal bleeding (overt or autopsy or imaging confirmation)

 

 

 

 





































9. Detailed description of procedure / processes: The study will be conducted after obtaining clearance from the ethical committee of the Institute and permission from the appropriate authority and PIS/ consent from patients. A total of 455 ACS patients presenting to department of cardiology ,KMC Manipal will be selected by simple random sampling as per inclusion and exclusion criteria. All patient will receive standard treatment as per guidelines according to patients clinical condition as per treating doctor’s advice. PCI will be performed by standard techniques. All coronary angiograms, sign of bleeding and investigations will be reviewed. Patients will be defined as HBR if they will fulfill at least 1 major or 2 minor criteria using the ARC-HBR criteria. Patients with 1 minor criteria will be considered as non-HBR. To evaluate the incremental prognostic value of multiple coexisting criteria while accounting for the binary nature of ACR-HBR definition , patients will be stratified according to the number of times they fulfill the ARC-HBR definition: 1 (1 x HBR), 2 (2 X HBR), 3 (3 X HBR), 4 time or more (4 X HBR).Bleeding risk will be calculated at the time of PCI. Baseline and procedural characteristics, including the available ARC-HBR criteria, will be systematically obtained using standardizes forms at the time of indexed hospitalization for PCI.

PRECISE-DAPT score is a simple 5 item score using age, creatinine clearance, haemoglobin, white-blood-cell count, and previous spontaneous bleeding. Age cut off taken was 50 – 90 years, hemoglobin 10 – 12 g/dL, upper cut off of creatinine clearance was 100 mL/min, white blood cell count range was between 5 – 20 thousand per microliter. A bedside web based calculator was used to calculate risk points for each item and the total points added. Total score ranges from 0 to 100 points. Prior bleeding gives 26 points on the nomogram while creatinine clearance of 0 has 25 points. Hemoglobin 10 g/dL and white blood cell count of 20,000 gives a score of 15 points each, while age of 90 or above gives a score of 19. All these together make a total score of 100 as the highest score possible. Patients with PRECISE DAPT score of > 25 was considered as High bleeding risk and < 25 was considered as low bleeding risk. Both score will be compared and bleeding risk will be assessed at the end of study.

 Data will be collected on follow up at 6 months after PCI. Patient will be followed up at 6 months for any Major adverse cardiac events (peri procedural in hospital and post discharge bleeding, as well as all cause mortality and post discharge myocardial infarction). For those patients who do not follow up at 6 months or suffer untoward event, telephonic contact will be attempted using the contact number recorded in patient records. During the telephonic contact, questions will be asked regarding patient’s current symptoms, cardiac events requiring admission and/or intervention(s) after index hospitalization, time to such event(s), if any, and medication adherence. If patient is not alive at the time of telephonic contact, attempt will be made to ascertain the timing of patient’s demise after index hospitalization, and possible cause of death based on symptoms, medical opinion (if patient was hospitalized) received by patient by-stander and circumstances surrounding death. A log of the call made including date and time and the name of the respondent will be recorded in a standard format.

 

 

10. Outcome measures:

 

1. Primary End Points- Rate of major bleeding at 6 months, defined as the composite of peri-procedural in hospital bleeding or post discharge bleeding.

The primary endpoint of the study was the rate of major bleeding at 6 months, defined as the composite of peri-procedural in-hospital bleeding or post-discharge bleeding. Peri-procedural in hospital bleeding was identified according to the data definitions of the National Cardiovascular Data Registry CathPCI Registry (version 4.4) as any bleeding occurring during hospitalization for the index PCI associated with a hemoglobin drop >_3 g/dl, blood transfusion, or requiring procedural intervention or surgery at the bleeding site. Post-discharge bleeding was defined as a bleeding event requiring either hospitalization or blood transfusion.

2. Secondary End Points- Individual components of the primary bleeding end point represented by peri procedural in hospital and post discharge bleeding, and all cause mortality and post discharge myocardial infarction and MACE-in hospital at 6 months follow up.

11. Potential risks and benefits : Minimal risk is involved in the study ,as required parameters are assessed from routine/standard tests.
Patient may not have direct benefit by participating in the study.

Result obtained from this study will help us in identification and management of patients at high bleeding risk including patients with severe comorbid conditions undergoing PCI, factors predicting severity, and clinical outcomes and safety and effectiveness of drugs and devices and drug regimens for patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention. Such data will be instrumental in better management of future patients undergoing PCI.

12. Ethical considerations and methods to address issues :
This is a prospective observational study and does not involve any tests or interventions as a part of the study. Approval from institutional ethics committee will be taken prior to patient recruitment. Written informed consent will be taken from all participants and only giving consent will be included in the study. To ensure confidentiality, indirect identifiers will be used for study participants. All the required parameters needed for the study will be obtained from their routine tests. We obtain basic information regarding patient’s clinical status and it is not expected to cause any adverse impact on patient. Also, there is no additional cost to be borne by patient or the hospital for the purpose of this study. All follow up data required for the study will be collected at their routine follow up visits.
 

13. Budget (give details) and proposed funding source :

 

Since it is an observational study, all the investigations and interventions are done as a part of standard level of care, there is no additional budget or funding required.

 

 

14. Review of literature (within 1000 words) : 

 

 

Hemorrhagic complications have emerged as an independent risk factor for subsequent mortality in patients with acute coronary syndromes (ACS) and in those undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), representing a hazard equivalent to or greater than that for myocardial infarction (MI)5,6,7,8,9,10,11. Major bleeding also considerably prolongs the hospital stay and increases resource consumption, representing an important source of excess expenditures12. Minimizing bleeding is therefore an important objective in the management of patients with ACS.

 

To facilitate risk stratification of patients, multiple scoring systems have been developed, albeit using heterogeneous cohorts and different definitions13,14,15,16,17,18. As a result, ambiguity about how to effectively identify patients at high bleeding risk (HBR) remains, and it is reflected in the varying inclusion criteria adopted by the completed and ongoing trials in such patients19,20,21,22,23.

Recently, the Academic Research Consortium for High Bleeding Risk (ARC-HBR) provided a consensus document with a list of clinical criteria classified as either major or minor to identify patients at HBR among those undergoing PCI24. There is compelling evidence in favour of a strong association between post-PCI bleeding complications and the risk of short- and long-term mortality10,11,25,7,6. Data from the U.S. National Cardiovascular Data Registry consisting of more than 3 million PCI procedures showed that peri-procedural bleeding, is responsible for approximately 12% of in-hospital mortality26.

Consequently, the need for bleeding avoidance strategies involving optimized peri-procedural management and tailored antithrombotic therapy has been emphasized, with guidelines endorsing the use of algorithms to predict the patient’s bleeding risk and inform decision making27,28. However, risk scores are intrinsically influenced by the characteristics of the studies used for their development, and this limits their prognostic value when applied to a diverse real-world group of patients29 .Yet, most of the validated and widely accepted risk assessment tools for bleeding have shown comparable discrimination, which, on the whole, points toward the shortcomings of basing clinical judgment only on a limited number of risk parameters.

This high prevalence is in contrast with that observed in 2 validation cohorts for the PRECISE-DAPT (Predicting Bleeding Complications in Patients Undergoing Stent Implantation and Subsequent Dual Antiplatelet Therapy) score, where only 23% of patients were classified as HBR. Similarly, another recent randomized trial of antiplatelet therapy reported that approximately 20% of the enrolled study group was at HBR according to the PARIS (Patterns of Non-Adherence to Anti-Platelet Regimen in Stented Patients) score30.

We sought to compare the ARC HBR DEFINITION and PRECISE-DAPT scores for the assessment of bleeding risks in INDIAN patients receiving DAPT Post PCI and compare predictive performance between the 2 bleeding risk scores.

trial_public_title: Genetic and hormone level testing in PCOS
trial_scientific_title_: Non-classic 21-Hydroxylase deficiency among women with PCOS: Study from western Indian population
trial_sponsors: proportion of PCOS patients with NCCAH
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	Inj. Tetracosactide acetate stimulated LC-MS/MS steroid and steroid precursor panel; next generation sequencing 	Patients will be called once for collection of venous blood samples for FSH,LH, prolactin, testosterone, TSH and for genetic analysis for CYP21A2 gene (targeted next generation sequencing) following which Inj. Tetracosactide acetate 250 mcg of (ACTH 1-24) will be given intramuscularly and blood samples will be drawn at an interval of 60 mins for steroid panel by LC-MS/MS including but not limited to 11-deoxycortisol, cortisol, 11-deoxycorticosterone, corticosterone, Progesterone, 17-hydroxyprogesterone, 21-Deoxycortisol, androstenedione and Testosterone, cortisone, DHEA and DHEAS. This is a one-time, one-visit intervention only.
trial_description:
Non-classic 21-hydroxylase deficiency (NCCAH) is one of the most common autosomal recessive disorders and is caused due to mutations in CYP21A2 gene with 20-50% residual enzyme activity. These present with subtle clinical features of hyperandrogenism like hirsutism, menstrual irregularities and infertility, and hence, are differential diagnoses of polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Older studies from India have shown NCCAH to be present amongst 1-5% of women with PCOS or hirsutism. However, the diagnosis of these patients was based on clinical and biochemical characteristics without genetic confirmation. The aim of this study was to estimate the prevalence of NCCAH amongst women with PCOS from western India.

All women > 18 years of age diagnosed with PCOS based Rotterdam criteria will be screened for inclusion in the study. Patients will be called during the follicular phase of the menstrual cycle if having regular cycles or on a random day if the patient is amenorrhoic blood samples will be drawn for FSH, LH, prolactin, testosterone and TSH following which Inj. Tetracosactide acetate 250 mcg of (ACTH 1-24) will be given intramuscularly and blood samples will be drawn at an interval of 60 mins for steroid panel by LC-MS/MS including 11-deoxycortisol, cortisol, 11-deoxycorticosterone, corticosterone, Progesterone, 17-hydroxyprogesterone, 21-Deoxycortisol, androstenedione and Testosterone, cortisone, DHEA and DHEAS. Venous blood sample will also be taken for Genetic analysis for CYP21A2 gene for targeted next generation sequencing. The values of steroid panel so obtained will be analysed to evaluate the differences in patients with PCOS vs those with NCCAH.

trial_public_title: An open label, non-invasive single centric study to validate 710_Blood Pressure Monitor machine for standard use
trial_scientific_title_: An open label, non-invasive single centric study to validate 710_Blood Pressure Monitor POC in adult population
trial_description:
The same arm sequential method of the 2019 AAMI/ESH/ISO Universal Standard is applied, which includes 2 entry BP measurements (reference R0 and test device
T0) followed by 4 reference measurements (R1, R2, R3, R4) taken alternately with 3 test device measurements (T1, T2, T3) and one measurement by Ormon device (O1). All measurements to be taken on the left arm. The observers were blinded to each other’s readings and the test device results. The supervisor recorded the test device measurements and checked the observers’ measurements.
In case of disagreement between the observers, additional pairs of measurements were performed. A maximum of 8 pairs of BP determinations was allowed after
which the subject was excluded.

trial_public_title: Study to evaluate safety and efficacy of TAVI -Transcatheter Aortic Valve (Crea Aortic Valve)
trial_scientific_title_: A prospective, multi-centre, single arm, pivotal clinical study to determine the safety and effectiveness of Crea Aortic Valve for use as a Replacement Aortic Heart Valve
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	Crea Aortic Valve 	Crea Aortic Valve Self Expanding Transcatheter Aortic Valve Frequency- Once Duration- Lifetime
trial_co_investigators: Bhagya Narayan Pandit, Bishav Mohan, Prof S K Dwivedi, Vijay Kumar Trehan, Praveen Chana, Manik Chopra, Siddhant Bansal, Amit Sharma
trial_description: To evaluate safety and performance outcomes following treatment with the study device in subjects at high surgical risk, as determined by the heart team who require replacement of their native aortic valve due to symptomatic, severe, aortic valve stenosis (AS).High surgical risk is defined as predicted risk of surgical mortality ≥8% at 30 days, based on the Society of Thoracic Surgeons (STS) risk score and other clinical comorbidities unmeasured by the STS risk calculator. The total planned study duration is 20 months, which includes an initial recruitment period of 6 months and follow-up period of 12 months and 02-month’s time for post study analysis and site close outs.

trial_public_title: effect of Mobile Radiations on the heart parameters and efficacy of Envirochip to neutralize this effect.
trial_scientific_title_: Observational Study to analyse the effect of Mobile Phone Radiations on Heart Rate Variability Parameters and efficacy of Envirochip in improving the same.
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	Envirochip 	As we are surrounded by many Manmade radiations which are emitted by Mobile Phone, Laptop, Wi-Fi Router etc. We want to see the impact of these radiations on HRV and efficacy of Envirochip in neutralizing this effect. Participants will be exposed to only naturally occurring radiations which they are facing on a day-to-day basis. The Source of these radiations can be their mobile phone, nearby wi-fi router, nearby Mobile tower, etc.
trial_description:
The use of Electronic gadgets such as mobile phones, computers/laptops constitutes an integral part of human day-to-day activity in offices and businesses. Therefore, their use cannot be stopped. These electronic gadgets emit electromagnetic radiations, which is believed to be hazardous to human health. World Health Organization/International Agency for Research on Cancer has classified radiofrequency electromagnetic fields as possibly carcinogenic to humans (Group B).

 

The Enviro Chip, developed by Synergy Environics, India, is a radiation harmonizing chip which when fixed on electronic gadgets at specific locations claims to neutralize the harmful effect of these radiations on the user.

 

Envirochip has been tested for improving the EEG of the Mobile Phone User and by Max healthcare for improving the pulse rate, as per defined protocol.

 

In this study, we want to analyze the effect of Mobile Phone Radiations on Heart Rate Variability Parameters and efficacy of Envirochip in improving the same, partially or fully.

 

The study will be conducted as per the defined protocol. All the Ethical and regulatory guidelines will be followed.

trial_public_title: Clinical study on Yesaka Plus Tablets in Type 2 Diabetic Patients
trial_scientific_title_: A Double blind, Multi-center, Randomized, Prospective, Interventional, Placebo controlled, Clinical study to Evaluate Efficacy and Safety of Yesaka Plus Tablets in patients diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes mellitus
trial_intervention_name: Intervention Arm	Drug	Other than Classical		(1) Medicine Name: Yesaka Plus Tablet contains, Reference: NA, Route: Oral, Dosage Form: Gutika/Vati/Ghana Vati/Tablets, Dose: 1200(mg), Frequency: bd, Bhaishajya Kal: Adhobhakta, Duration: 90 Days, anupAna/sahapAna: Yes(details: water), Additional Information: -
trial_indication: (1) ICD-10 Condition:E119||Type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications. Ayurveda Condition: PRAMEHAH,
trial_co_investigators: Shishr Pande, Deepa Patil, Shripat Kore
trial_description: It is a double blind, multi-center, randomized, prospective, interventional, placebo controlled, clinical study to evaluate efficacy and safety of Yesaka Plus Tablets in patients diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes mellitus. The study will be conducted at 4 centers in India. As per computer generated randomization list, subjects will be either randomized to Yesaka Plus Tablets group or Placebo Tablet group in 1:1 ratio. Subjects will be asked to consume given medication in a dose of 2 tablets twice daily orally after meals with water for 90 days. The primary objectives of the study will be to assess change in HbA1C% (Glycosylated Hemoglobin) over a period of three months and between the groups and change in FBS and PPBS on monthly basis over 90 days (Before and after treatment) and between the groups. The secondary objectives will be to assess change in Fasting Serum Insulin, change in HOMA-IR score, change in clinical symptoms of Type 2 DM, difference in the number of rescue medication required by each group, monthly change in weight and BMI, change in quality of life assessed on QOLID, change in global assessment for overall change by subject and investigator, tolerability of study drugs and change in Laboratory parameters on day 0, day 30, day 60, day 90.

trial_public_title: Prevalence of thyroid disease in diabetics.
trial_scientific_title_: Prevalence of Subclinical hypothyroidism in Indian patients with Type 2 diabetes mellitus-a real world multi-centric study
trial_indication: (1) ICD-10 Condition: E02||Subclinical iodine-deficiency hypothyroidism, (2) ICD-10 Condition: E118||Type 2 diabetes mellitus with unspecified complications,
trial_description:
Prevalence of Subclinical hypothyroidism in Indian patients with Type 2 diabetes mellitus - a real world multi-centric study

Diabetes and thyroid disorders are the two most common disorders amongst endocrine diseases. Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (DM) may disturb thyroid metabolism by disturbing the levels of thyroid hormones in the plasma.

trial_public_title: Project on wearable watch for measuring Heart rate, Blood pressure and temperature
trial_scientific_title_: An open-label, single Center, observational Clinical evaluation study to assess the accuracy of a WearAble device for viTal sign monitoring as Compared to the conventional digital devices (ACCUWATCH)
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	GoQii wearable band 	the device can be strapped to participant in order to check body temperature, heart rate and blood pressure
trial_indication: (1) ICD-10 Condition: R688||Other general symptoms and signs,
trial_description:
This study is a prospective, open-label, single centric, comparative, observational study on patients visiting the clinical site for their routine examinations. Patients who match the inclusion and exclusion criteria and who sign the written informed consent form will be enrolled into the study. 

The objective of this study is to evaluate the accuracy of wearable device (GoQii) for monitoring vital signs (Body Temperature, Blood Pressure and Heart Rate) in comparison to conventional digital device vital signs (Body Temperature, Blood Pressure and Heart Rate) readings.

After the patient wear the test product wearable device (GoQii), at baseline (0 hour) vital signs reading will be recorded from the wearable device (GoQii) display and the readings from comparator conventional digital device by the study investigator. These readings will be entered into the application based eCRF by the investigator or study coordinator. With a difference of 3 hours each two more vital signs readings (3rd and 6th hour) will also be obtained from the test product wearable device (GoQii) and the comparator conventional digital device by the study investigator or study coordinator and recorded in the application based eCRF. The study will be completed after 6 hours and patient can leave the hospital.

trial_public_title: Study to compare the efficacy and safety of novel Methylcobalamin Spray with Sublingual Methylcobalamin Tablet in treatment of subjects with vitamin B12 deficiency.
trial_scientific_title_: A Prospective, Open-Label, randomized, Clinical study to compare the efficacy and safety of novel Methylcobalamin Nasal Spray with Sublingual Methylcobalamin Tablet in the treatment of subjects with vitamin B12 deficiency.
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	NASO B12 Nasal Spray 	One spray containing 250 µg of Methylcobalamin in each nostril (total two sprays containing 500 mcg of Methylcobalamin) will be administered on alternate-day as follows on Day 1, 3 and 5.
trial_indication: (1) ICD-10 Condition: E00-E89||Endocrine, nutritional and metabolic diseases,
trial_description:
This clinical trial is a prospective, open label, randomized, clinical study to compare the efficacy and safety. A total 30 Male and Female subjects, whose Vitamin B12 level is <200pg/mL will be enrolled.

Subject will be screened according to the study inclusion and exclusion criteria within 2 weeks prior to randomization (Day 0). Subject meeting all the inclusion criteria and none of the exclusion criteria will be invited to participate in the study. Subjects who provide informed consent to participate in the study will  be randomized either to the Methylcobalamin NASO B12 Spray or Sublingual Methylcobalamin Tablet in a 1:1 ratio. Each group will consist of 15 randomized subjects.

trial_public_title: Treatment of vitamin B 12 deficiency in adult subjects by using Methylcobalamin (250 Ang/Spray) nasal spray
trial_scientific_title_: A prospective, open-label, single-arm clinical study to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of novel Methylcobalamin nasal spray in the treatment of subjects with vitamin B 12 deficiency.
trial_indication: (1) ICD-10 Condition: D519||Vitamin B12 deficiency anemia, unspecified,
trial_description: Vitamin B12, also known as cobalamin, anti-pernicious anaemia factor, Castle’s extrinsic factor, or animal protein factor is the largest and most complex of all the vitamins. It is necessary for the formation of blood cells, nerve sheaths, and various proteins. It is also involved in fat and carbohydrate metabolism. It is also essential for DNA synthesis, cellular energy production, and growth. Vitamin B12 is also required in the synthesis of folate polyglutamates (active coenzymes required in the formation of nerve tissue) and in the regeneration of folic acid during red blood cell formation. Cobalamin Deficiency is defined as B12 levels <200pg/mL and SCCD or Subclinical Cobalamin Deficiency refers to B12 levels <400pg/mL.Nasal Methylcobalamin therapy has been recently introduced as an innovative route for the systemic availability of B12 due to the large surface area, porous endothelial membrane, high total blood flow, the avoidance of first-pass metabolism, and ready accessibility of the route. Methylcobalamin is absorbed rapidly, safely, and consistently from the nasal cavity after intranasal administration. Further, the intranasal formulation has overcome the drawbacks of the intramuscular formulation. It is convenient and painless. Nasal Methylcobalamin Spray can eliminate the need for the assistance of Nursing staff or Paramedics for injection and reduce the overall cost of therapy. It would also facilitate ease of administration and improve compliance.The aim of study is to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of novel methylcobalamin nasal spray in the treatment of subjects with vitamin B12 deficiency. the primary end points are to Change in Vitamin B12 levels on day 8 from Baseline [i.e. after 07 doses of Nasal B12] and Change in Vitamin B12 levels on day 14 from Baseline [i.e. after 14 doses of Nasal B12].

trial_public_title: Smart Watch Study for Monitoring Vital Signs
trial_scientific_title_: An open-label, single Center, observational Clinical evaluation study to assess the accuracy of a WearAble device for viTal sign monitoring as Compared to the conventional digital devices
trial_indication: (1) ICD-10 Condition: R688||Other general symptoms and signs,
trial_description:
BACKGROUND AND RATIONALE 

 

With the advancement of technology, the smartwatch market is thriving in the mobile industry space as direct-to-consumer wearables and medical devices start to blend in creating the possibility of monitoring personal health.

Measuring vital signs such as temperature, heart rate, etc has become easy and newer technology of adding ECG sensors and Spo2 sensors are being introduced in Wearable medical devices (WMDs). WMDs are continuous ambulatory monitoring devices of human physical activities and behaviours, as well as physiological and biochemical parameters during daily life or in a clinical environment, with the advantage of minimizing discomfort and interference with normal human activities. WMDs are worn by users and the device measures the relevant vital sign parameter through biosensors or other analog measurement technology. This analog data is later converted into digital format and stored on a mobile app or cloud database platform.



For this study we intend to evaluate the accuracy of novel wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) for vital sign monitoring by comparing the results of this device with conventional digital devices vital sign readings. 



RESEARCH OBJECTIVES 

 

To evaluate the accuracy of wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) for vital sign monitoring as compared to conventional digital devices. 

 

STUDY DESIGN 

 

This study is a Medical Device Clinical Evaluation Study. The study is designed to be a prospective, open-labeled, single center, comparative, observational study.  

 

The study is planned to be conducted at Diabetes Care Hormone Clinic, Ahmedabad and Principal Investigator will be Dr Banshi Saboo, MD (Diabetologist) 

 

SAMPLE SIZE 

 

We plan to enroll 50 patients in this study to evaluate the accuracy of wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) for vital sign monitoring. 


 

STUDY TREATMENTS 

 


This is Observational Clinical Evaluation Study to assess the Vital Signs in out-patient population. Treatment if applicable will be provided by the physician as part of routine clinical practice. The novel wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) readings will not be used to take Clinical Decisions for the patients. 



STUDY OUTCOME 

 

To assess accuracy of novel wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) for vital sign monitoring (Body Temperature, Blood Pressure, Heart Rate, ECG and Oxygen Saturation (SpO2)) comparing it with conventional readings recorded by digital devices.  

Accuracy will be determined by comparing the vital sign readings obtained simultaneously from novel wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) and conventional digital devices. Comparison will be performed using paired samples t-tests and Pearson’s correlation coefficients. 

 

SAFETY OUTCOME 

 

The novel wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) is a wellness device and no therapeutic or clinical decisions will be taken based on the novel wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) readings. However, Adverse events (AE) or serious adverse events (SAE), either spontaneously reported by the patient, or noticed by the clinician will be recorded during the study 


 

STUDY METHODOLOGY 

 

This study is a prospective, open-label, single centric, comparative, observational study on patients visiting the clinical site for their routine examinations. Patients who match the inclusion and exclusion criteria and who sign the written informed consent form will be enrolled into the study.  

The objective of this study is to evaluate the accuracy of novel wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) for monitoring vital signs (Body Temperature, Blood Pressure, Heart Rate, ECG and Oxygen Saturation (SpO2)) in comparison to conventional digital device vital signs (Body Temperature, Blood Pressure, Heart Rate, ECG and Oxygen Saturation (SpO2)) readings. 


After the patient wear the test product wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0), at baseline (0 hour) vital signs reading will be recorded from the novel wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) display and the readings from comparator conventional digital device by the study investigator. These readings will be entered into the application based eCRF by the investigator or study coordinator. With a difference of 3 hours each two more vital signs readings (3rd and 6th hour) will also be obtained from the test product wearable device (GoQii Smart Vital 2.0) and the comparator conventional digital device by the study investigator or study coordinator and recorded in the application based eCRF. The study will be completed after 6 hours and patient can leave the hospital.

trial_public_title: Comparison of Glucose Monitoring in children and youth with Type 1 diabetes in India
trial_scientific_title_: Comparison of Flash Glucose Monitoring and Capillary Blood Glucose monitoring in children and youth with Type 1 diabetes in India
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	Flash Glucose Monitoring 	FGM systems consist of two components: a disposable sensor that measures interstitial glucose levels, and a receiver that displays and stores estimated mean values of glucose that have been standardized to capillary blood glucose levels for 6 month
trial_indication: (1) ICD-10 Condition: E109||Type 1 diabetes mellitus without complications,
trial_description:
Patients in each setting will be randomized to one of two groups: 1) FGM using the Libre 1 flash glucose monitor (intervention) for six months followed by usual capillary blood glucose monitoring four times per day (current local standard of care) for six months; or 2) Capillary blood glucose monitoring four times per day first (current local standard of care), followed by FGM using the Libre 1 flash glucose monitor (intervention) for six months (Figure 2). Each patient will serve as their own control. During intervention treatment periods, participants will be required to scan their sensor at least pre and post meals, before bed and upon waking, and occasionally during the night. They should be educated not to wait longer than 8 hours between scans (overnight). Parents should be advised to scan their child’s Libre before they go to bed to ensure all data is captured overnight.

            Both groups will receive intensive in-person or virtual group education about flash and capillary blood glucose monitoring at the time of enrolment and at the time of crossover. During intervention treatment periods, patients (with parent/guardian as appropriate) will have approximately one 30-minute in-person or virtual study visit at one month to assess how they have adapted to the FGM device and answer any questions. They will be asked to attend for approximately 30-minute in-person follow-up visits at three and six months.

After the 12 months follow up period (6 months intervention and cross over), all patients will enter a 6-month observation period during which they will be offered the opportunity to again use FGM (the Libre 1 flash glucose monitor) if they desire. Any insulin adjustments and dietary re-education or adjustment based on review will be undertaken as per local standard of care.
